posts.snippet: NASA's Artemis II astronauts completed a full simulation of their crewed Moon flyby, rehearsing re-entry and recovery procedures ahead of next year's launch — a key milestone in returning humans to lunar space.

Using JWST spectroscopy, astronomers confirmed the most distant galaxy yet observed, seen just 290 million years after the Big Bang — a new record that pushes the frontier of cosmic distance and early-universe astrophysics.
